sections.paragraph:
Νεφθαλίμ, ὁ indecl. (also-είμ; נַפְתָּלִי; Gen 30:8; 49:21; Judg 4:6, 10 al. With μ at the end Is 8:23 and occas. [Gen 49:21; Dt 33:23; Judg 4:6 B] as v.l.; JosAs 22:5; 24:8 cod. A [p. 76, 20 Bat.]; Demetr.: 722 Fgm. 1, 3 al. [-είμ]; Philo, Somn. 2, 36. On this s. PKatz, TLZ 61, ’36, 281.—In Jos., Ant. 1, 305 Νεφθάλεις and 2, 181 Νεφθάλις) Naphtali, Hebrew tribe (and its ancestor) φυλὴ Ν. Rv 7:6. Its land γῆ Νεφθαλίμ Mt 4:15 (Is 8:23). (τὰ) ὅρια Ν. vs. 13; Lk 4:31 D.—BHHW II 1287.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

occas. occas. = occasionally

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TLZ TLZ = Theolog. Literaturzeitung—List 6

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ν.̠

  1. = Nestle/Aland, Novum Testamentum Graece, 27th ed. Other editions are indicated by a superscript—List 1, beg.
  2. = neuter; note (in bibliographical references)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 670
